ROLLING HILLS REHAB CTR

CCN: 525430 · SPARTA, WI 54656 · Monroe County

Address
14400 CTY HWY B, SPARTA, WI 54656

Setting

3 /5
★★★★★
CMS Overall Rating

CMS 5-Star Ratings

CMS rates every Medicare/Medicaid-certified nursing home on four domains. The Overall rating is driven primarily by Health Inspection results, then adjusted up or down by Staffing and Quality Measures.

Overall Rating
Composite of Health Inspection, Staffing, and Quality Measures.
★★★★★ 3/5
Health Inspection
Based on the three most recent standard surveys, with more weight on recent results.
★★★★★ 3/5
Staffing
Weighted combination of RN and total nurse staffing hours per resident per day, case-mix adjusted.
★★★★★ 4/5
Quality Measures
15 resident-level quality measures split between long-stay and short-stay (post-acute) residents.
★★★★★ 2/5
Quality Measures breakdown
Long-Stay Quality Measures ★★★★★ 2/5
Short-Stay Quality Measures — Data not available.

Staffing & Workforce

Direct-care staffing is the strongest operational driver of quality in nursing homes. Values are hours per resident per day, derived from payroll-based journal (PBJ) submissions. "Case-mix" adjusts for resident acuity; "Adjusted" is the CMS rating-input value.

Role Reported Case-mix expected Adjusted Federal floor
Total nurse
All nursing staff combined: RN + LPN + Aide
 4.21 3.61 4.50 ≥ 3.48
Registered Nurse (RN)
Licensed RN hours. Strongest driver of clinical outcomes.
 0.93 0.63 1.00 ≥ 0.55
Licensed Practical Nurse (LPN)
LPN/LVN hours. Often handles medication administration.
 0.74 0.80 0.79 —
Nurse aide
CNA hours. Bulk of direct resident care — bathing, feeding, mobility.
 2.53 2.18 2.71 —
Licensed (RN + LPN)
Combined licensed nurse coverage.
 1.67 — — —
Physical therapist
Rehabilitation therapist hours — important for post-acute / rehab admissions.
 0.01 — — —

Federal minimums (phasing in under the CMS 2024 minimum staffing rule) shown for reference. RN: 0.55 hrs/resident/day. Total nurse: 3.48 hrs/resident/day.

Weekend staffing

Total nurse (weekend)
3.92
hrs / resident / day
RN (weekend)
0.55
hrs / resident / day
Total nurse (adjusted, weekend)
4.20
hrs / resident / day

Weekend under-staffing is a common quality-of-care concern — adverse events are more frequent when licensed coverage drops.

Staff turnover

Total nursing staff turnover
New facility — insufficient data.
RN turnover
New facility — insufficient data.
Administrators departed
0
last 12 months

Resident acuity

Nursing Case-Mix Index
1.28
higher acuity than avg
Case-Mix Index Ratio
0.93
facility / state ratio

Health Inspections

CMS weights three inspection cycles to compute the Health Inspection rating: the most recent (50%), the second most recent (33%), and the oldest (17%). Each standard-survey deficiency is assigned a score based on scope and severity; complaint-survey findings and revisit scores are added to produce the cycle total.

Cycle Date Total defs. Standard Complaint Deficiency score Revisits Total score
Cycle 1 (most recent) 2025-02-20 10 9 1 56 1 56
Cycle 2/3 (prior) 2024-01-11 6 4 2 28 1 28
Total weighted health score
49.0
lower is better

Deficiencies (20)

Individual survey findings. Scope/severity uses the CMS A–L matrix: letters further down the alphabet indicate greater harm and wider scope, up through J–L (immediate jeopardy).

20 D/E/F Actual harm — potential for minor
Tag Description Scope/Severity Survey date Corrected
0609 Timely report suspected abuse, neglect, or theft and report the results of the investigation to proper authorities. D 2026-01-20 2026-02-06
0600 Protect each resident from all types of abuse such as physical, mental, sexual abuse, physical punishment, and neglect by anybody. D 2025-02-20 2025-03-18
0609 Timely report suspected abuse, neglect, or theft and report the results of the investigation to proper authorities. D 2025-02-20 2025-03-18
0610 Respond appropriately to all alleged violations. D 2025-02-20 2025-03-18
0640 Encode each resident’s assessment data and transmit these data to the State within 7 days of assessment. D 2025-02-20 2025-03-18
0688 Provide appropriate care for a resident to maintain and/or improve range of motion (ROM), limited ROM and/or mobility, unless a decline is for a medical reason. E 2025-02-20 2025-03-18
0689 Ensure that a nursing home area is free from accident hazards and provides adequate supervision to prevent accidents. D 2025-02-20 2025-03-18
0759 Ensure medication error rates are not 5 percent or greater. D 2025-02-20 2025-03-18
0761 Ensure drugs and biologicals used in the facility are labeled in accordance with currently accepted professional principles; and all drugs and biologicals must be stored in locked compartments, separately locked, compartments for controlled drugs. D 2025-02-20 2025-03-18
0880 Provide and implement an infection prevention and control program. F 2025-02-20 2025-03-18
0610 Respond appropriately to all alleged violations. D 2024-08-20 2024-09-05
0689 Ensure that a nursing home area is free from accident hazards and provides adequate supervision to prevent accidents. D 2024-08-20 2024-09-05
0550 Honor the resident's right to a dignified existence, self-determination, communication, and to exercise his or her rights. D 2024-01-11 2024-02-07
0583 Keep residents' personal and medical records private and confidential. D 2024-01-11 2024-02-07
0812 Procure food from sources approved or considered satisfactory and store, prepare, distribute and serve food in accordance with professional standards. E 2024-01-11 2024-02-07
0880 Provide and implement an infection prevention and control program. D 2024-01-11 2024-02-07
0585 Honor the resident's right to voice grievances without discrimination or reprisal and the facility must establish a grievance policy and make prompt efforts to resolve grievances. E 2022-12-01 2022-12-22
0604 Ensure that each resident is free from the use of physical restraints, unless needed for medical treatment. D 2022-12-01 2022-12-22
0689 Ensure that a nursing home area is free from accident hazards and provides adequate supervision to prevent accidents. E 2022-12-01 2022-12-22
0759 Ensure medication error rates are not 5 percent or greater. D 2022-12-01 2022-12-22
